event: Self-inflicted gunshot injury to the abdomen
Laparotomy at another hospital
Perforation in the antrum of the stomach antrum and multiple small bowel perforations
Primary surgical repair of stomach and small bowel perforations
Bilateral lateral (zone 2) retroperitoneal hematomas
Transfer to ICU
Development of pulmonary embolism (PE) and disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC)
Management with blood products
Recovery from PE and DIC
Discharge from ICU
Presentation to local health center with left iliac fossa pain watery stool reduced hemoglobin level and raised serum C-reactive protein (CRP)
Abdominal CT scan showing large left-sided well-enhanced retroperitoneal collection with contrast extravasation into the retroperitoneal space
Diagnosis of left ureteral injury with urinoma formation and colonic fistula
Referral to trauma center
Cystoscopy with retrograde pyelogram showing complete transection of the left upper ureter with contrast extravasation into the retroperitoneal space
Left percutaneous nephrostomy and insertion of left-sided pigtail drain
Treatment with antibiotics
Clear urine draining from the urinoma
Repeat CT scan showing reduction in size of left-sided urinoma collection
Insertion of another pigtail drain
Drainage of minimal dark reddish fluid
Removal of pigtail drain
Discharge from hospital
Return to hospital for elective surgery
Cystoscopy showing extra-luminal compression of the bladder from the pelvic hematoma
Intraoperative findings of 2.5 cm segment of dense fibrosis in the upper ureter
Left end-to-end ureteric anastomosis with excision of the intervening injured ureter
Spatulation of the ends with a tension-free waterproof anastomosis over a double J stent
Colonoscopy with methylene blue instillation through the pigtail drain
Removal of left double J stent
Follow-up cystoscopy
